name: Honduras

outbound_official.note: no official Honduras government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The Secretaría de Relaciones Exteriores y Cooperación Internacional (sreci.gob.hn) is the competent authority but its website has persistent SSL certificate failures preventing access. The Instituto Nacional de Migración (inm.gob.hn) visa page consistently times out. No cached or mirror version of an official Honduras government page listing countries where Honduran ordinary-passport holders can travel visa-free, visa on arrival, or with e-visa/ETA was found. Third-party aggregators (Henley, Wikipedia, visaindex.com, etc.) exist but are explicitly excluded per methodology. Searches on sreci.gob.hn, inm.gob.hn, conmigho.sreci.gob.hn, asuntosconsulares.inm.gob.hn, and sre.gob.hn returned no accessible outbound visa lists for Honduran nationals.

visa_free.notes: US citizens do not need a visa for tourism stays up to 90 days. Passport valid at least 6 months required. COVID-19 vaccination proof no longer required as of December 2024.

Canadian citizens do not require a visa for tourist or business visits up to 90 days. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months from date of entry. Extension of up to 30 days may be requested through the INM prior to expiry of initial authorisation (maximum total stay 120 days). The 90-day period is not guaranteed; duration is at immigration officers' discretion. Land border entry requires payment of USD $3 or equivalent in lempiras.

Under the Central American Free Mobility Agreement (CA-4), nationals of Guatemala, El Salvador, and Nicaragua may enter Honduras using either a national ID (DNI) or passport. The 90-day stay counts cumulatively across the CA-4 territory.

Colombian nationals do not require a visa to enter Honduras since reciprocal visa exemption agreement effective September 12, 2007. Stays for tourism, transit, or business up to 90 days. International yellow fever vaccination certificate required (administered at least 10 days before entry).

Bolivian ordinary, official, and diplomatic passport holders are exempt from visa requirements under the Acuerdo sobre Supresión de Visas signed 20 September 2023 (Acuerdo Ejecutivo No. 14-DGAJTC-2023). Entered into force 17 March 2024. Stay limited to 90 days cumulatively per calendar year for tourism/visit purposes.

visa_policy.notes: Honduras has no e-visa system. Tourists may stay up to 90 days, extendable by an additional 30 days at the INM. Citizens of all nationalities (other than CA-4) must hold a passport valid at least 6 months. Honduras is a CA-4 member: a single entry authorisation is valid across Guatemala, El Salvador, Honduras, and Nicaragua for a cumulative 90 days. UK visa exemption was cancelled effective 15 August 2024 — UK ordinary passport holders now require a visa (narrow exception for UK citizens domiciled in the Cayman Islands unverified). The INM official website (inm.gob.hn) lists visa categories but a comprehensive enumerated list of all visa-free nationalities was not retrievable from the official page during research sessions. A broader list of approximately 84 visa-free jurisdictions is cited by aggregators (including USA, Canada, EU/Schengen states, Japan, South Korea, Australia, New Zealand, most of South America, GCC states, etc.) but those specific enumerations could not be independently verified against the official INM source for this validation run. Canada visa-free status (90 days, tourism and business) confirmed from Canadian government official travel advice (travel.gc.ca, last updated 23 June 2026). Costa Rica: ordinary passport holders enter without a consular visa under Acuerdo N.003-SG-2023 (signed 24 October 2023), but most must present a criminal background certificate (certificación de antecedentes penales) — classified as conditional access. A June 2026 amendment (Acuerdo-003-SG-2026, effective 22 June 2026) exempts Costa Ricans who also hold a valid US or Canadian visa or residency from the criminal background certificate requirement; this sub-group is classified as documentary-conditional.

visa_types.name: Visa de Turismo / Tourist and Business Visa (Single Entry)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreigners wishing to travel to Honduras for recreational, tourism, business, or family purposes. Covers short-term stays for non-remunerative activities.
visa_types.notes: Required only for nationalities that do not enjoy a bilateral visa exemption with Honduras. Many nationalities (USA, Canada, EU/Schengen, etc.) do not need a visa for stays up to 90 days under the CA-4 agreement or bilateral exemptions. For those who do require a visa, the single-entry tourist/business visa is valid for 60 days. Payment must be via money order in USD. Cruise ship passengers in Roatán with limited stays are exempt. Canadian passport holders are visa-exempt for stays up to 90 days.

visa_types.name: Visa de Turismo / Tourist and Business Visa (Multiple Entry)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreigners for recurring tourism, business, or family visits to Honduras over a one-year period.
visa_types.notes: Multiple-entry version of the tourist/business visa. Valid for 1 year from issuance. Individual stays subject to standard immigration discretion at point of entry. Payment via money order in USD only. Processing may take approximately 4–8 weeks when prior authorization is required.

visa_types.name: Visa de Turismo y/o Negocios (Business)
visa_types.purpose: Covers short-term business activities such as meetings, negotiations, conferences, and commercial visits to Honduras without engaging in local employment.
visa_types.notes: Business visits are covered under the same tourist/business visa category per the Honduras Embassy in Canada. Nationals of countries with visa exemptions (USA, Canada, EU/Schengen, etc.) do not require a visa for business stays up to 90 days. The Canadian government confirms business visits up to 90 days are visa-exempt for Canadian citizens.

visa_types.name: Visa de Tránsito (Transit Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals transiting through Honduras en route to a third country.
visa_types.notes: Transit visa fee is USD $30.00 per the official consular fee schedule (Decree 263-2011 / VUCA Regulation). Required for nationalities that need a visa to enter Honduras and are transiting through Honduran airports or land borders.

visa_types.name: Residencia por Trabajo (Work Residency)
visa_types.purpose: Granted to foreign nationals employed in Honduras under a valid employment contract or with an employer commitment letter, allowing them to reside and work legally in the country.
visa_types.notes: Applicants must present an employment contract or employer commitment letter (notarized if not drafted in Honduras). Required documents include passport valid at least 1 year, 2 passport photos, authenticated birth certificate, criminal background check (with fingerprints), and health certificate. Application fee is USD $150. Per the Colombia embassy page, work visa applications must be processed directly in Honduras through the Instituto Nacional de Migración (INM). All documents require Spanish translation with originals plus two copies.

visa_types.name: Permiso/Residencia de Estudios (Student Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals intending to pursue formal education at an accredited institution in Honduras.
visa_types.notes: Student permits are required for any foreign national studying in Honduras. Per the Canadian government's travel advice, students must obtain the permit from immigration authorities upon arrival in Honduras. Per the Colombia embassy page, student visa (visa de estudios) applications must be processed directly in Honduras through the Instituto Nacional de Migración (INM). Specific fees and required documents must be confirmed with INM directly (inm.gob.hn, callcenter@inm.gob.hn).

visa_types.name: Residencia de Pensionado (Pensioner/Retiree Residency)
visa_types.purpose: Grants long-term residency to foreign retirees who can demonstrate a permanent pension income from abroad of at least USD $1,500 per month.
visa_types.notes: Minimum monthly pension income of USD $1,500 (or equivalent in local currency) from abroad, received via a Honduran financial institution. Requires bank reference and pension provider verification (both notarized and authenticated). Application fee is USD $150. After obtaining residency, naturalization may be pursued after continuous qualifying residency. Documents must be submitted with Spanish translations and originals plus two copies.

visa_types.name: Residencia de Inversionista (Investor Residency)
visa_types.purpose: Grants long-term residency to foreign nationals who make a qualifying investment of at least USD $50,000 in a lawful economic activity in Honduras.
visa_types.notes: Minimum investment of USD $50,000 in any lawful economic activity in Honduras (including certificates, securities, or State bonds). Also requires a certificate of deposit of not less than USD $5,000 from the Central Bank of Honduras as guarantee. A technical feasibility study and investment schedule must be submitted. Application processed through a Honduran lawyer registered with the National Bar Association. Covers the principal investor and immediate family. After 3 years of continuous residency, naturalization may be pursued.

visa_types.name: Residencia Rentista (Rentier/Passive Income Residency)
visa_types.purpose: Grants long-term residency to foreign nationals who demonstrate stable passive or rental income of at least USD $2,500 per month from domestic or foreign sources.
visa_types.notes: Minimum passive/rental income of USD $2,500 per month (or equivalent in national currency), received via a Honduran financial institution. Income can originate abroad or within Honduras. Requires notarized income documentation authenticated by a provincial notary chamber. Application fee is USD $150. Documents must be submitted with Spanish translations and originals plus two copies.

visa_types.name: Residencia por Matrimonio / Vínculo Familiar (Family Reunification Residency)
visa_types.purpose: Grants residency to foreign nationals who are married to or have close family ties with Honduran citizens or permanent residents.
visa_types.notes: Category covers spouses and close family relations (matrimonio/vínculo familiar). Per the Colombia embassy page, family relationship visas (vínculo familiar) must be processed directly in Honduras through the INM. Application fee is USD $150. Required documents include authenticated marriage or family relationship certificates, criminal background check, health certificate, and valid passport. All documents require Spanish translation.

visa_types.name: Visa Diplomática (Diplomatic Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to diplomatic and consular officials, government representatives, and international organization officials holding valid diplomatic passports, for official missions to Honduras.
visa_types.notes: Issued free of charge to holders of diplomatic passports on official missions. Covers diplomatic and consular officials and government representatives accredited to Honduras. Members assigned as members of a diplomatic/consular mission must obtain the appropriate diplomatic or consular visa even if their nationality otherwise benefits from a visa exemption.

visa_types.name: Visa de Cortesía (Courtesy Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to holders of special or service passports traveling on official missions, including cultural and sports delegations and certain international organization personnel.
visa_types.notes: Issued free of charge to special/service passport holders on official missions. Also covers official cultural and sports delegations and certain international organization personnel who do not qualify for a full diplomatic visa.

visa_types.name: Visa Oficial (Official Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to consular officials, public officials, and international organization representatives holding official passports, UN Laissez-passer, or OAS passports.
visa_types.notes: Issued free of charge to consular officials and international organization representatives with official documents (official passports, UN Laissez-passer, or OAS passports). Distinct from the diplomatic visa category.

cbi.notes: Honduras does not operate a Citizenship-by-Investment (CBI) program. No official CBI programme was found on any Honduran government domain. Investment-based residency is available (see rbi), and naturalization requires 3 years of continuous residency plus language/civics requirements.

programs.name: Residente Inversionista (Investor Resident)
programs.notes: Minimum investment of USD $50,000 in any lawful economic activity in Honduras (including certificates, securities, or State bonds). Also requires a certificate of deposit of not less than USD $5,000 issued by the Central Bank of Honduras as guarantee. A technical feasibility study and investment schedule must be submitted. Application processed through a Honduran lawyer registered with the National Bar Association. Investment covers principal investor and immediate family. After 3 years of continuous residency, naturalization may be pursued subject to language/civics requirements.

programs.name: Residente Pensionado (Pensioner Resident)
programs.notes: Must demonstrate permanent, lawful, and stable pension income generated abroad of not less than USD $1,500 per month or equivalent in national currency. Pension must be received via a Honduran financial institution. Supporting authenticated documents required.

programs.name: Residente Rentista (Rentier Resident)
programs.notes: Must demonstrate permanent, lawful, and stable rental or passive income (generated abroad or within Honduras) of not less than USD $2,500 per month or equivalent in national currency. Income must be received via a Honduran financial institution. Supporting authenticated documents required.

agreements: CA-4 (Central America-4 Free Mobility Agreement - Guatemala, El Salvador, Honduras, Nicaragua)
Honduras-Bolivia Visa Suppression Agreement (Acuerdo Ejecutivo No. 14-DGAJTC-2023, in force 17 March 2024) - covers ordinary, official, and diplomatic passports
Honduras-Costa Rica Bilateral Visa Suspension Agreement (Acuerdo N.003-SG-2023, signed 24 October 2023) - ordinary passport holders; criminal background certificate required on entry
Honduras-Costa Rica Amendment (Acuerdo-001-SG-2026, 24 January 2026) - grants Costa Rican nationals category A status under the 2023 agreement
Honduras-Costa Rica Amendment (Acuerdo-003-SG-2026, effective 22 June 2026) - exempts Costa Ricans holding valid US or Canadian visa/residency from criminal background certificate requirement

conditional_access.conditions: Chinese nationals holding valid Diplomatic Passports, Service Passports, or Public Affairs Passports (Pasaportes de Asuntos Públicos) are exempt from visa requirements for entry, exit, transit, or stay in Honduras for up to 30 days from date of entry. Those wishing to stay beyond 30 days or for purposes other than tourism/transit must obtain a visa in advance. Holders assigned as members of a diplomatic/consular mission must still obtain the appropriate Diplomatic or Consular visa. Agreement signed Beijing, 12 June 2023 (Acuerdo Ejecutivo No. 09-DGAJTC-2023). NOTE: Chinese ordinary passport holders require a consular visa to enter Honduras. El Heraldo fact-check (citing INM) confirms that the bilateral exemption does NOT extend to ordinary passports; only diplomatic/service/public affairs passport holders are covered. An 'Authorization for Entry Without Visa' from the INM can be issued for specific cases (official missions, humanitarian grounds, work) but this is not a standard visa-free entry right for ordinary passport holders.

conditional_access.credential: certificación de antecedentes penales (criminal record certificate from the Costa Rican judiciary) — unless holder also has a valid US or Canadian visa or residency (exempt per Acuerdo-003-SG-2026, effective 22 June 2026)
conditional_access.conditions: Costa Rican ordinary passport holders may enter Honduras without a consular visa under the bilateral visa suspension agreement signed 24 October 2023 (Acuerdo N.003-SG-2023). Standard rule: entry requires presentation of a criminal record certificate (certificación de antecedentes penales) issued by the Costa Rican judiciary. Diplomats and transport workers are exempt from the certificate requirement. The 2026 amendment (Acuerdo-003-SG-2026, effective 22 June 2026) further exempts Costa Ricans who hold a valid US or Canadian visa or residency from the criminal background certificate requirement — they must present documentation of that credential at border control. Costa Ricans without US/Canadian credentials still must present the criminal background certificate. Maximum stay duration not confirmed from official source. Classified as conditional access (documentary condition) for both sub-cases.

validation_notes: 2026-06-29: CONFIRMED USA visa-free (90 days) — travel.state.gov fetched successfully. COVID-19 vaccination proof no longer required per December 2024 update. No other changes to US entry rules. Minor notes update only.

2026-06-29: CONFIRMED Canada visa-free (90 days, tourism and business) — travel.gc.ca fetched successfully, page last updated 23 June 2026. Added land border entry fee (USD $3) to notes. No change to visa-free classification.

2026-06-29: CONFIRMED UK requires a visa — gov.uk/foreign-travel-advice/honduras fetched successfully. Confirms 'You must have a visa to visit Honduras.' Cruise ship passengers under short pass still exempt. No change.

2026-06-29: CONFIRMED Colombia visa-free (90 days) — Honduras Embassy Colombia page fetched successfully. Agreement from 12 September 2007 confirmed. Added yellow fever vaccination certificate requirement to notes.
2026-06-29: NO CHANGE to Bolivia (Acuerdo No. 14-DGAJTC-2023) — TSC library returned HTTP 403 again; prior confirmation preserved as no contradictory evidence found.

2026-06-29: UPDATED Costa Rica conditional_access — new Acuerdo-003-SG-2026 (effective 22 June 2026) exempts Costa Ricans holding a valid US or Canadian visa or residency from the criminal background certificate requirement. They must show proof of US/Canadian credential. Those without such credential still require the criminal background certificate. Both remain conditional_access (documentary condition). Source: Infobae report (news, not official). TSC PDF (Acuerdo-001-SG-2026, Acuerdo-003-SG-2026) returned HTTP 403 preventing verification of full official text.
2026-06-29: UPDATED agreements list — added Acuerdo-001-SG-2026 (24 January 2026) and Acuerdo-003-SG-2026 (effective 22 June 2026) for Costa Rica.
2026-06-29: NO CHANGE to CA-4 (Guatemala, El Salvador, Nicaragua) — no contradicting evidence found.
2026-06-29: NO CHANGE to RBI programs — inm.gob.hn/residencias.html timed out; prior figures retained.
2026-06-29: NO CHANGE to CBI, fast_track — no programs found.
2026-06-29: CONFIRMED no e-visa or visa-on-arrival system in Honduras.

PRIOR NOTE (2026-06-27): ADDED Canada to visa_free; ADDED Costa Rica to conditional_access; CONFIRMED China ordinary passport holders require a visa; CONFIRMED UK visa required; CONFIRMED USA, Colombia, CA-4; ADDED Bolivia; ADDED travel.gc.ca to sources.

PRIOR NOTES (2026-06-20 and earlier): ADDED Bolivia to visa_free (Acuerdo Ejecutivo No. 14-DGAJTC-2023). UPDATED UK exemption cancellation note (effective 15 August 2024). CLARIFIED China conditional_access. ADDED visa_types and outbound_official fields.
gaps: The official INM visa page (inm.gob.hn/visas.html) timed out during re-validation; a full enumerated list of visa-free nationalities could not be scraped directly from the official source.

TSC library (tsc.gob.hn/biblioteca) and TSC PDF files returned HTTP 403 during this validation run; PDFs of executive agreements (Acuerdo-001-SG-2026, Acuerdo-003-SG-2026, Acuerdo Ejecutivo No. 14-DGAJTC-2023) could not be fetched to verify Bolivia, Costa Rica, or China agreements against primary documents.
No official e-visa system found; Honduras does not offer e-visas as of the validation date.
No official digital nomad visa or fast-track skilled worker scheme found on any Honduran government domain.
Processing time for investor residency could not be confirmed from the INM official page.

The comprehensive list of approximately 84 visa-free jurisdictions cited by aggregators (including EU/Schengen states, UK pre-August 2024, Australia, Japan, South Korea, etc.) could not be fully verified against the official INM source due to server unavailability.
UK narrow Cayman Islands exemption (cited by Cayman News Service, August 2024) — official document not fetched; not added to conditional_access.

Credential-based entry rule (US/Schengen/Canada valid visa holders admitted visa-free to Honduras): widely reported by aggregators. NOT recorded in conditional_access because the underlying Honduran executive decree has not been located or fetched from any official Honduran government domain.

Costa Rica conditional_access: source for June 2026 change is a news article (Infobae, 25 June 2026) and La Prensa Honduras, not an official government domain. The TSC PDFs (Acuerdo-001-SG-2026, Acuerdo-003-SG-2026) returned 403 — official documents need re-checking when TSC library becomes accessible. Maximum stay for Costa Ricans not confirmed from official source.

Diplomatic/service/official passport exemptions for additional countries (Belarus, Cuba, Dominica, Egypt, Fiji, Grenada, Guyana, Haiti, India, Jamaica, Kenya, Libya, Montenegro, Morocco, Pakistan, Papua New Guinea, Peru, Philippines, Serbia, Suriname, Thailand; diplomatic only for Palestine) reported by Wikipedia — could not be verified against official Honduran sources.
Dominican Republic: Wikipedia lists 30-day visa-free stay (shorter than standard 90 days). Not confirmed from official Honduran source; not recorded.
ZEDE e-residency programs (e.g., Prospera) are not nationally recognised immigration instruments and were excluded.
Dual citizenship allowance under Honduras law not confirmed from official INM source.

outbound_official.found = false: Honduras's SRECI (sreci.gob.hn) has SSL certificate failures and INM (inm.gob.hn) times out. No official Honduras government-published list of outbound visa-free access was located.
Student permit fee and processing time not confirmed from official source. Medical visa category not confirmed from any official Honduran source. Working holiday visa: no bilateral agreement found.

Acuerdo-Ejecutivo-3-DGAJTC-2024, Acuerdo-Ejecutivo-005-DGAJTC-2024, Acuerdo-Ejecutivo-13-DGAJTC-2025, Acuerdo-Ejecutivo-04-DGAJTC-2025 appeared in prior TSC search results but content could not be fetched (HTTP 403) — subject matter unknown, may or may not relate to visa exemptions.
